opcoes: a) She speaks English very well.
b) She speaks very well English.
c) She very well speaks English.
d) She speaks English well very.
questoes.explicacao: Correct order: Subject + verb + object + adverb (very well).

opcoes: a) Where you live?
b) Where do you live?
c) Where does you live?
d) Where live you?
questoes.explicacao: Question: Where + do/does + subject + verb?

opcoes: a) He doesn't likes coffee.
b) He don't like coffee.
c) He doesn't like coffee.
d) He not like coffee.
questoes.explicacao: Negative: doesn't + base form (like).

opcoes: a) Always she arrives late.
b) She arrives always late.
c) She always arrives late.
d) She arrives late always.
questoes.explicacao: Adverbs of frequency come before the main verb (except 'to be').

opcoes: a) What time does the train leave?
b) What time the train leaves?
c) What time leaves the train?
d) What time does the train leaves?
questoes.explicacao: Correct: What time + does + subject + verb (base form)?

opcoes: a) I yesterday went to the cinema.
b) I went to the cinema yesterday.
c) I went yesterday to the cinema.
d) Yesterday I went to the cinema.
questoes.explicacao: Time expressions usually go at the end (or beginning). Both B and D are correct, but B is more common. The test expects B.

opcoes: a) She is a very intelligent girl.
b) She is a girl very intelligent.
c) She is very intelligent a girl.
d) She is a very intelligent girl.
questoes.explicacao: Adjectives come before nouns: a very intelligent girl.

opcoes: a) Are you from Brazil?
b) You are from Brazil?
c) Is you from Brazil?
d) Are from Brazil you?
questoes.explicacao: Yes/No questions with 'be': Are you...?

opcoes: a) I am not tired.
b) I not am tired.
c) I am no tired.
d) I aren't tired.
questoes.explicacao: Negative of 'be': I am not.

opcoes: a) He has a car blue.
b) He has a blue car.
c) He has car blue.
d) He blue has a car.
questoes.explicacao: Adjective before noun: a blue car.

opcoes: a) Why are you crying?
b) Why you are crying?
c) Why are crying you?
d) Why you crying?
questoes.explicacao: Question with 'be': Why + are + subject + verb-ing?

opcoes: a) They can to play tennis.
b) They can play tennis.
c) They can plays tennis.
d) They can playing tennis.
questoes.explicacao: Modal verbs (can) + base form.

opcoes: a) He cannot speak French.
b) He can not speak French.
c) He don't can speak French.
d) He can't speaks French.
questoes.explicacao: Negative of can: cannot (can't) + base form.

opcoes: a) They often go to the beach.
b) They go often to the beach.
c) Often they go to the beach.
d) They go to the beach often.
questoes.explicacao: Adverbs of frequency before main verb: often go.

opcoes: a) How much does it cost?
b) How much it costs?
c) How much costs it?
d) How much does it costs?
questoes.explicacao: Question: How much + does + subject + cost?

opcoes: a) She is more tall than her sister.
b) She is taller than her sister.
c) She is tall than her sister.
d) She is more taller than her sister.
questoes.explicacao: Comparative: taller (one syllable).

opcoes: a) I have never been to London.
b) I have been never to London.
c) I never have been to London.
d) I have been to London never.
questoes.explicacao: Adverbs of frequency (never) after auxiliary: have never been.

opcoes: a) Who did you see?
b) Who you saw?
c) Who did you saw?
d) Who you did see?
questoes.explicacao: Question word as object: Who + did + subject + verb (base form).

opcoes: a) She told me a secret.
b) She told a secret me.
c) She me told a secret.
d) She told to me a secret.
questoes.explicacao: Indirect object (me) before direct object: told me a secret.

opcoes: a) I haven't seen him today.
b) I have not seen him today.
c) I haven't saw him today.
d) Both A and B are correct.
questoes.explicacao: Present perfect negative: haven't/has not + past participle.

opcoes: a) They are building a new house.
b) They are building a house new.
c) They building a new house.
d) They are build a new house.
questoes.explicacao: Present continuous: are + verb-ing + object.

opcoes: a) Whose car is that?
b) Whose is that car?
c) Whose car that is?
d) Both A and B are correct.
questoes.explicacao: Both 'Whose car is that?' and 'Whose is that car?' are possible.

opcoes: a) She likes very much chocolate.
b) She likes chocolate very much.
c) She very much likes chocolate.
d) She likes chocolate much very.
questoes.explicacao: Correct: like + object + very much.

opcoes: a) I didn't see nobody.
b) I didn't see anybody.
c) I saw nobody.
d) Both B and C are correct.
questoes.explicacao: Double negatives are incorrect. 'I didn't see anybody' and 'I saw nobody' are correct.

opcoes: a) He gave to me a gift.
b) He gave a gift to me.
c) He gave me a gift.
d) Both B and C are correct.
questoes.explicacao: Both 'He gave a gift to me' and 'He gave me a gift' are correct.

opcoes: a) How long have you been waiting?
b) How long you have been waiting?
c) How long have you been wait?
d) How long you been waiting?
questoes.explicacao: Present perfect continuous question: How long + have + subject + been + verb-ing?

opcoes: a) She works in a hospital.
b) She work in a hospital.
c) She does work in a hospital.
d) She is work in a hospital.
questoes.explicacao: Simple present, 3rd person: she works.

opcoes: a) I usually have breakfast at 7.
b) I have usually breakfast at 7.
c) I have breakfast usually at 7.
d) Usually I have breakfast at 7.
questoes.explicacao: Adverb of frequency before main verb: usually have.

opcoes: a) What are you doing?
b) What you are doing?
c) What are you do?
d) What you doing?
questoes.explicacao: Present continuous question: What + are + subject + verb-ing?

opcoes: a) She doesn't have any money.
b) She doesn't have no money.
c) She hasn't no money.
d) She doesn't has any money.
questoes.explicacao: Negative: doesn't have + any.

opcoes: a) This is the book that I bought.
b) This is the book that I bought it.
c) This is the book I bought it.
d) This is the book what I bought.
questoes.explicacao: Relative clause: that I bought (no pronoun after).

opcoes: a) I don't know where she lives.
b) I don't know where does she live.
c) I don't know where she live.
d) I don't know where lives she.
questoes.explicacao: Indirect question: subject + verb (no inversion).

opcoes: a) She asked me where I was going.
b) She asked me where was I going.
c) She asked me where I going.
d) She asked me where am I going.
questoes.explicacao: Reported question: subject + verb (no inversion).

opcoes: a) Do you like ice cream?
b) Like you ice cream?
c) You like ice cream?
d) Does you like ice cream?
questoes.explicacao: Simple present question: Do + subject + verb?

opcoes: a) I haven't got a car.
b) I don't have a car.
c) I have not got a car.
d) All are correct.
questoes.explicacao: All are correct: have got / have / do have negatives.

opcoes: a) She is always late.
b) She always is late.
c) Always she is late.
d) She is late always.
questoes.explicacao: With 'be', adverbs of frequency come after the verb: is always.

opcoes: a) I want that you come.
b) I want you to come.
c) I want that you to come.
d) I want you come.
questoes.explicacao: Verb + object + to-infinitive: want you to come.

opcoes: a) How many people were at the party?
b) How many people was at the party?
c) How many people at the party?
d) How many people there were at the party?
questoes.explicacao: Correct: How many + plural + were?

opcoes: a) I have never seen such a beautiful sunset.
b) I never have seen such a beautiful sunset.
c) I have seen never such a beautiful sunset.
d) I have seen such a beautiful sunset never.
questoes.explicacao: Adverb of frequency after auxiliary: have never seen.

opcoes: a) It's too hot to go out.
b) It's too hot for go out.
c) It's too hot to going out.
d) It's too hot for going out.
questoes.explicacao: Too + adjective + to-infinitive.

opcoes: a) Which one do you prefer?
b) Which one you prefer?
c) Which one does you prefer?
d) Which one you do prefer?
questoes.explicacao: Question: Which + one + do + subject + verb?

opcoes: a) He didn't say anything.
b) He didn't say nothing.
c) He said nothing.
d) Both A and C are correct.
questoes.explicacao: A and C are correct; B has double negative.

opcoes: a) She speaks three languages fluently.
b) She speaks fluently three languages.
c) She fluently speaks three languages.
d) She speaks three languages fluent.
questoes.explicacao: Verb + object + adverb.

opcoes: a) He is enough old to drive.
b) He is old enough to drive.
c) He is old to drive enough.
d) He is enough to drive old.
questoes.explicacao: Adjective + enough + to-infinitive.

opcoes: a) Whom did you see?
b) Who did you see?
c) Whom you saw?
d) Both A and B are correct.
questoes.explicacao: In informal English, 'who' is often used; 'whom' is formal.

opcoes: a) I saw a film very interesting.
b) I saw a very interesting film.
c) I saw very interesting a film.
d) I saw a film interesting very.
questoes.explicacao: Adjective before noun: a very interesting film.

opcoes: a) She told that she was tired.
b) She said that she was tired.
c) She told me that she was tired.
d) Both B and C are correct.
questoes.explicacao: 'Say' + (that) clause; 'tell' + indirect object + clause.

opcoes: a) How far is it to the station?
b) How far it is to the station?
c) How far is to the station?
d) How far there is to the station?
questoes.explicacao: Question: How far + is + it + to...

opcoes: a) I'm going to buy a new car next week.
b) I'm going to buy next week a new car.
c) I'm going next week to buy a new car.
d) Next week I'm going to buy a new car.
questoes.explicacao: Correct order: I'm going to buy a new car next week.

opcoes: a) She is the woman who lives next door.
b) She is the woman which lives next door.
c) She is the woman whom lives next door.
d) She is the woman what lives next door.
questoes.explicacao: 'Who' for people as subject.
